utility_notes: preserve positive-class default=1 coverage; classification utility depends on not washing out the minority default class
PAY_0 through PAY_6 are the strongest default-risk indicators, with PAY_0 the most important recent repayment-status signal
LIMIT_BAL and recent payment amounts such as PAY_AMT1/PAY_AMT2 carry important inverse risk signal
BILL_AMT1 through BILL_AMT6 are highly dependent monthly bill-amount sequence features and should support realistic credit-utilization structure
target default payment next month must never appear in theta even though it is the label

pair_priors: recent and prior repayment status form the strongest default-risk trajectory
repayment status is serially dependent across adjacent billing months
delinquency status should evolve coherently over the monthly sequence
monthly bill amounts are highly autocorrelated and anchor account balance continuity
late-sequence bill amounts have very strong adjacent-month dependence
credit limit constrains bill scale and utilization patterns
repayment status remains strongly serially dependent later in the sequence
last two repayment-status months should remain coherent
bill amount sequence has strong adjacent-month continuity
middle-month bill balances should not drift independently
bill amount autocorrelation remains high across later months
payment amount should be plausible relative to current bill amount
monthly payment should remain aligned with corresponding bill amount
lower credit limits and repayment status jointly describe default-risk strata
age and marital status are demographic fields with strong dependency
education group is associated with credit-limit distribution
recent delinquency and recent payment amount jointly affect utility signal

shape: monetary columns are right-tailed; synthetic data can flatten large BILL_AMT* and PAY_AMT* extremes
PAY_AMT1 through PAY_AMT6 are zero-inflated and nonnegative; zero mass and long tails both matter
BILL_AMT1 through BILL_AMT6 can be negative and zero-heavy; clipping all negatives to zero would distort shape
repayment-status columns contain rare high-delay categories such as 5-8; rare categories should not be dropped or amplified
EDUCATION and MARRIAGE contain rare coded values including 0, and these should stay plausible
trend: BILL_AMT1 through BILL_AMT6 are highly autocorrelated monthly balances and should not be sampled independently
PAY_0 through PAY_6 form an ordered repayment-status trajectory with strong adjacent-month dependence
payment amounts should remain plausible relative to bill amounts and repayment status
LIMIT_BAL constrains bill scale, payment behavior, and utilization patterns
demographic dependencies such as AGE with MARRIAGE should remain coherent without dominating utility
privacy: configured privacy columns are LIMIT_BAL, SEX, EDUCATION, MARRIAGE, and AGE
LIMIT_BAL plus demographics can create narrow strata, especially when combined with exact bill/payment amounts
high-resolution BILL_AMT* and PAY_AMT* values can act as quasi-identifiers
repayment-status trajectories across six months can identify unusual delinquency patterns
current code-derived bounds require broad col_ps for 23 features, but col_ps must not include all feature columns
utility: dropping PAY_0 or later PAY_* status columns will substantially weaken default classification utility
class 1 is the minority default class at about 22%; synthetic data should preserve positive-class coverage
using only demographics as utility focus is too weak and risks learning proxy demographics instead of repayment behavior
bill amounts alone have weaker direct target association than repayment status and credit/payment behavior
col_u should usually be PAY_0, PAY_2, LIMIT_BAL, or a recent payment amount such as PAY_AMT1

seed_theta_examples.reason: fidelity seed follows shape risks for LIMIT_BAL, BILL_AMT1-6, PAY_AMT1-3, recent repayment status, and AGE; col_ps omits late repayment status and late payment amounts rather than copying other seeds
trend seed follows the repayment-status trajectory and adjacent bill-balance priors; its privacy scope deliberately emphasizes monetary/time-series quasi-identifiers and omits demographic columns

privacy seed uses the broadest default col_ps: configured demographic/credit columns, repayment trajectory, early-to-late bill amounts, and payment amounts; it still omits BILL_AMT6 so it is broad without becoming full-feature privacy

utility seed follows the real target signal: PAY_0, PAY_2-6, LIMIT_BAL, and recent payment amounts dominate default prediction; col_ps omits later bill balances rather than reusing the fidelity/privacy combination
